parsed_review.summary: CP00-809 (RP26 Enterprise SaaS Hardening, risk A, 10 units RP26.P06.M05.S29-RP26.P06.M06.S08) is a descriptor-only permission/audit binding tail + synthetic fixture transition pack. Verified by direct source inspection only (Read/Grep/Glob; no Bash/commands executed). (1) Binding ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP809_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:409) is internally consistent (pack_id CP00-809, risk A, 10 units, range RP26.P06.M05.S29-RP26.P06.M06.S08, upstream CP00-808, next CP00-810 / RP26.P06.M06.S09) and chains cleanly from CP808 whose next_pack_id=CP00-809 / next_subphase RP26.P06.M05.S29 (registry.js:402-403). (2) Coverage reconciles exactly with the prompt: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_CP809_REQUIREMENTS (registry.js:1889) deliverable_counts implementation:8/security_audit:2, phase RP26.P06:10, micro M05:2/M06:8, micro_title Permission And Audit Binding:2/Synthetic Fixture Set:8, and required_section_rows M05=PERMISSION_MATRIX_EVIDENCE_HANDOFF_ROWS.slice(6,8)=[Command rerun, Risk register update] / M06=PERMISSION_MATRIX_ROWS.slice(0,8)=[Permission matrix row, View/Search/Mutation/Export-download/Share/AI retrieval decision binding, Audit hint fields]. These titles map 1:1 onto the live plan units docs/closeout-pack-plan/closeout-pack-plan.json:398-519 (S29,S30 in M05; S01-S08 in M06), with deviation_from_plan=false; the evidence-handoff split is clean (CP808 consumed slice(0,6), CP809 takes slice(6,8)). validateCoverage (validators.js:130) and validateDescriptor (validators.js:166) perform real, non-vacuous assertions (pack/risk/unit/first/last/duplicate/RP26-only, all four distribution maps, per-section row count+membership, authority flags, and per-row descriptor_only=true / runtime_execution=false / no tenant/SSO/SCIM/secret payloads / no runtime permission/audit/UI/API/identity/cross-tenant/key material). model.test.js:906-970 runs these against the real plan file (planPack reads the live plan; CP00-809 present with matching risk/units/range) and asserts errors==[]. (3) Descriptor createEnterpriseSaasCp809PermissionAuditSyntheticFixtureTransitionDescriptor (service.js:1526) sources from the CP808 descriptor (source_descriptor=EnterpriseSaasCp808PermissionAuditBindingBridgeDescriptor) and carries the correct authority_boundary (claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-810) at service.js:1546-1551. (4) No-write/no-real-data/runtime-closed boundaries enforced at multiple layers: ENTERPRISE_SAAS_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION (registry.js:426) is spread into every freezeResult (service.js:59-67) and createCaseSet rows default descriptor_only=true/runtime_execution=false with all tenant/sso/scim/secret payload flags false (service.js:263-296); validateDescriptor rejects any drift; the contract's persisted row block (contract:37700-37715) shows all runtime/leak/trust flags false. (5) Hermes used only for a descriptor-only evidence packet (createEnterpriseSaasCp809HermesEvidencePacket service.js:1827: writes_product_state=false, emits_runtime_receipt=false, receipt_shape descriptor_only_enterprise_saas_no_write); Claude packet (service.js:2412) is read_only=true, blocks_pack_on_p0_p1_p2=true, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false. (6) Contract/plan/README/index/script mutually consistent: contract current_pack & latest_pack=CP00-809 (lines 55,71), latest_projection.descriptor=EnterpriseSaasCp809... (line 30785), projections.cp809 present (line 30234), validation.valid=true (line 37720); README #cp00-809 accurate (README.md:61); index.js wildcard re-exports resolve the validate-script named CP809 imports (scripts/validate-rp26-enterprise-saas-hardening-contract.mjs:4-10). The manifest makes no premature claim: production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, production_ready_descriptor_verified=false, status=in_progress, pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending, valid_review_receipts=[]/invalid_review_attempts=[] - consistent with the prompt authority boundary (enterprise trust closed, human approval required, Hermes not embedded in product runtime, Claude read-only and non-final). Unrelated dirty files (scripts/progress-control-room-data.mjs, tools/progress-control-room/index.html, workbook/*, the .docx, docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md, the unrelated LDIP c-ldip-01-invalid-attempt result) are correctly listed as unrelated_dirty_files_preserved and excluded from active pack scope. Review readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON, non-zero stdout (1445 bytes), exit 0; no auth-failure/0-byte/malformed/fenced/tool-call-shaped output and no prior CP809 review evidence exists. As read-only independent reviewer (not final approver) I find no P0/P1/P2 issues; the bounded descriptor-verified completion claim is supported. One P3 documents that downstream closeout artifacts and the validation-command run are not yet present/executed - expected at this review stage and outside what a read-only review can confirm.

findings.title: Downstream closeout artifacts and validation-command evidence not yet present (expected at the Claude-review stage)
findings.details: manifest.required_pack_artifacts (line 227) lists command-evidence.json, claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json, and every included unit's evidence_refs point to artifacts/closeout-pack-claude-review/cp00-809/review-receipt.json plus those four files, but only manifest.json currently exists under docs/closeout-packs/cp00-809/ (Glob confirms) and the artifacts dir has no raw-output.json/review-receipt.json yet. pack_level_claude_review.status=review_pending with valid_review_receipts=[]. This is the expected state during the Claude review step: those artifacts, plus execution of the listed validation commands (npm run closeout-pack-plan:validate, npm run closeout-pack:validate, npm test, npm run validate, npm run spec:requirements:validate, npm run weighted:validate, npm run fullplan:validate, npm run goal:closeout:validate, npm run rp00:control-plane:validate, git diff --check), are produced/run by the closeout pipeline after this review. Being read-only, I verified the binding (registry.js:409), requirements/rows (registry.js:1889,666,694), descriptor and no-write/authority boundaries (service.js:1526, 263, 59), validators (validators.js:130,166,697,715), tests (model.test.js:906-970), contract promotion/projection/validation.valid (contract:71,30785,37720), live plan entry (plan line 398), README (README.md:61), and index re-exports by source inspection only; I did not and cannot execute commands or confirm downstream adjudication/inspection. No defect in the pack's current claims was found - the manifest correctly reports production_ready=false / runtime_ready=false / in_progress and does not overclaim. This mirrors the equivalent non-blocking P3 recorded for CP00-808.
findings.recommendation: Proceed with the normal closeout pipeline: run the listed validation commands, capture command-evidence.json, and generate claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, and construction-inspection.json; then obtain human final approval before any runtime or enterprise-trust opening (deferred to CP00-810 / RP26.P06.M06.S09). No code change required for this pack.
